round_summary: All agents coordinate on adding one cow while proposing a conditional restraint rule to pause expansion if pasture decline accelerates.
Agents coordinate on continued moderate expansion while proposing a conditional restraint norm tied to pasture decline.
All agents add one cow while proposing a shared conditional pause if pasture decline worsens, indicating coordination but limited immediate restraint.
Two agents advocate immediate restraint based on the grass threshold, while one agent adds despite acknowledging ecological risk and proposes a collective pause only if decline continues.
All agents reaffirm coordinated restraint in response to declining grass levels, reinforcing a shared conservation norm.
All agents signal restraint and coordinate around a shared no-growth norm in response to complete pasture depletion.
